$comment: Reference case for height_blend (result = saturate((heightA-heightB+blendFactor)/blendSharpness)). Single coefficient blendSharpness. heightB and blendFactor are pinned to 0 so the saturate argument is heightA/blendSharpness — a clean linear ramp whose slope is 1/blendSharpness. Canonical blendSharpness=0.15 differs from the library default 0.1 so the fitter recovers it from a cold start.
notes: 30-point sweep over heightA ∈ [-0.05, 0.30]. With heightB=blendFactor=0, the output is saturate(heightA/0.15): clamped to 0 below heightA=0, a linear ramp through the transition band heightA ∈ [0, 0.15], then clamped to 1. ≈13 samples sit in the ramp and fix the sharpness; 10 % relative tolerance reflects that the clamped tails carry no gradient.
